name: Tour of Lac de Lacanau
description: The Tour of Lac de Lacanau is an easy 19.9-mile mountain bike route with 251 feet of gain, offering scenic lake views and pine forests.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lacanau offers diverse terrain, situated between the Atlantic Ocean, a large freshwater lake, and the extensive pine forests of the Landes de Gascogne. The region is characterized by numerous winding paths through maritime pine forests, providing varied options for all skill levels. Adjacent to the coast, sandy beaches and dunes offer additional cycling opportunities, while the freshwater lake features scenic paths. This combination of forest, lake, and coastal environments defines the area's mountain biking landscape. Best mountain bike trails around Lacanau The most popular mountain bike trail is Tour of Lac de Lacanau, a 19.9 miles (32.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This easy route offers scenic lake views and passes through pine forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hourtin Lake Beach – Carcans-Plage loop from Le Huga, a moderate 22.3 miles (35.9 km) path. This route connects lake and coastal environments, providing varied scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Beach view – German track in the forest loop from Lacanau-Océan, a 15.9 miles (25.5 km) trail leading through pine forests with glimpses of the beach, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Mountain biking around Lacanau is defined by extensive pine forests, a large freshwater lake, and the Atlantic coast. The network offers options for different ability levels, from family-friendly routes to more technical trails. The routes in Lacanau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 2700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lacanau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Lacanau?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around Lacanau, catering to various skill levels. The region offers a diverse landscape of pine forests, a large freshwater lake, and the Atlantic coast, providing a wide range of options for riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Lacanau?
A: Mountain biking in Lacanau features diverse terrain. You'll primarily find winding paths through extensive maritime pine forests, offering a fragrant and shaded experience. Many routes also provide scenic views along the large freshwater &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lacanau Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and some trails venture near the coastal dunes and sandy beaches of the Atlantic Ocean.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Lacanau?
A: The mountain bike trails in Lacanau are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 350 reviews. Riders often praise the varied scenery, the extensive network of paths through the pine forests, and the options available for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Lacanau?
A: Yes, Lacanau is very welcoming for beginners and families. Over 50 easy mountain bike routes are available. The flat paths within the vast pine forest are particularly great for leisurely cycling with children. A popular easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995954505/tour-of-lac-de-lacanau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Lac de Lacanau&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic lake views and passes through pine forests.

Q: Are there any more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: While Lacanau is known for its accessible trails, there are 25 moderate mountain bike routes for those seeking a bit more challenge. These routes often feature longer distances and more varied elevation changes compared to the easy trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37583262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hourtin Lake Beach – Carcans-Plage loop from Le Huga&lt;/a&gt;, which connects lake and coastal environments.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Lacanau offer access to several natural features and landmarks. You can explore the vast pine forests, enjoy views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/965820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moutchic Beach&lt;/a&gt; and other lake beaches, or even spot wildlife in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cousseau Pond Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also provide glimpses of the Atlantic coast.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Atlantic Ocean?
A: Yes, some mountain bike trails in Lacanau-Océan lead through pine forests with occasional glimpses of the Atlantic coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35982144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach view – German track in the forest loop from Lacanau-Océan&lt;/a&gt; is an easy ride that takes you through the forest with beach views.

Q: Are dogs allowed on mountain bike trails in Lacanau?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on many of the natural trails in the pine forests around Lacanau, especially if kept on a leash. However, specific regulations might apply in protected areas like the Étangs de Cousseau Nature Reserve. It's always best to check local signage or park rules for the specific trail you plan to ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Lacanau?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Lacanau are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995954505/tour-of-lac-de-lacanau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Lac de Lacanau&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35251145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Among the pines – Le Moutchic loop from Le Moutchic&lt;/a&gt; is another popular moderate option that takes riders through the pine forests.

Q: Where can I rent a mountain bike in Lacanau?
A: Lacanau offers several bike rental services. You can find shops that provide mountain bikes, electric bikes, and equipment for children, along with repair services. These are typically located in the main town areas and near popular starting points for trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lacanau?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking in Lacanau, offering pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds than the peak summer season. The pine forests provide shade, making rides enjoyable even on warmer days, but checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants near the mountain bike trails in Lacanau?
A: Yes, Lacanau has various cafes, restaurants, and pubs, especially in the main town and around Lacanau Lake and Lacanau-Océan. Many trails are easily accessible from these areas, allowing for convenient stops before or after your ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41469222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moutchic Beach – Lacanau Harbour loop from Marie Curie&lt;/a&gt; passes by areas with amenities.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Lac de Lacanau suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this tour is largely considered easy and very suitable for all skill levels, including beginners and families with children. The minimal elevation gain and well-maintained cycle paths make it a pleasant experience. There's even a specific 18 km "Lion loop" section through the forest that is highlighted as very easy.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike tour?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of well-maintained cycle paths, often paved, winding through vast pine forests. While generally smooth, some sections might include short, sharp climbs and occasional sandy descents. More adventurous riders can find sandy tracks alongside the paved paths, which can be more challenging, especially with loose sand. Near lake beaches and campgrounds, soft sand can also make riding harder during peak season.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Tour of Lac de Lacanau?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the trail is easily accessible around Lacanau. Given its popularity and integration into a larger network, you'll find parking available near the lake's main access points, towns, and beaches like Grande Escoure and Le Moutchic, which serve as good starting points for the tour.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike tour?
faqs.answer: The Tour of Lac de Lacanau is approximately 32 km (20 miles) long. For an average mountain biker, it typically takes around 1 hour and 53 minutes to complete, allowing for a relaxed pace to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Tour of Lac de Lacanau is designed as a circular route, allowing you to start and end at the same point without needing to retrace your steps. This makes it convenient for exploring the entire lake.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the stunning Landes forest, primarily composed of maritime pines, offering the pleasant scent of pine and sea air. The route provides picturesque lake views, including a notable Lake view. Keep an eye out for a belvedere around km 15.6 and the Pointe du Bernos for exceptional panoramic views of the lake. You might also catch a View of the Lake in Morning Mist or a View of Lac d'Hourtin from Camping Le Tedey.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming or other activities along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Lac de Lacanau itself offers numerous opportunities for swimming and various water sports. The trail passes by beautiful beaches like Grande Escoure and Le Moutchic, providing perfect spots for refreshing breaks and a dip in the lake during your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Tour of Lac de Lacanau?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, but be prepared for warmer weather and potentially more soft sand near beaches due to increased tourist activity. The scent of pine trees and sea air is particularly invigorating during these seasons.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Lac de Lacanau dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on the cycle paths and forest trails in the region, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local signage for specific restrictions, especially near beaches or protected areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the Tour of Lac de Lacanau or the extensive network of cycle paths in the Médoc Atlantique region. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Does the Tour of Lac de Lacanau connect with other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this tour is part of an extensive network of over 400 km of cycle paths in the Médoc Atlantique region. Portions of the route also integrate with well-known paths such as the Voie Verte Bordeaux - Lacanau Océan and the Vélodyssée, a famous long-distance cycling route in France. Other intersecting paths include the Voie Verte Hourtin - Arès and the Piste Cyclable Lège-Lacanau.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the trail that are more challenging or require specific attention?
faqs.answer: While the tour is generally easy, some sections might feature short, sharp climbs, locally known as "coups de cul." Additionally, if you venture onto the sandy tracks running alongside the paved paths, or ride near lake beaches and campgrounds during the tourist season, you might encounter soft, loose sand, which can significantly increase the riding difficulty and require more effort.
